title: Cache Management API + LRU Eviction
description:
Implement cache management API and LRU (Least Recently Used) eviction policy.

**Scope:**
- Cache management API endpoints (clear, invalidate, stats)
- LRU eviction policy with configurable max size
- Cache statistics (hit/miss rate, size, memory usage)
- Manual cache invalidation by pattern/namespace

**Dependencies:**
- Requires MCPROXY-adt.2 (Multi-Tier) and MCPROXY-adt.3 (TTL) to be completed first

**Out of Scope:**
- Cache backend abstraction (handled by MCPROXY-wr8)
- Cache warming (handled by MCPROXY-010)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Cache management API implemented (clear, invalidate, stats)
- LRU eviction working with configurable limits
- Statistics endpoint returns cache metrics
- Manual invalidation works by pattern/namespace

title: TTL Management and Per-Namespace Limits
description:
Implement TTL (Time-To-Live) management with per-namespace limits and automatic expiration.

**Scope:**
- TTL support in cache operations (set with expiry)
- Background expiration checker (periodic cleanup)
- Per-namespace TTL limits and quotas
- TTL statistics and monitoring

**Dependencies:**
- Requires MCPROXY-adt.1 (Cache Core) to be completed first

**Out of Scope:**
- Adaptive TTL learning (handled by MCPROXY-s1j)
- Cache warming (handled by MCPROXY-010)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- TTL support in set() operations
- Automatic expiration of stale entries
- Per-namespace TTL configuration
- TTL statistics available (hit rate, expiration count)

title: Multi-Tier Cache Architecture
description:
Implement multi-tier cache architecture with global, session, and namespace tiers.

**Scope:**
- Design 3-tier cache hierarchy (global > session > namespace)
- Tier lookup logic (check namespace first, then session, then global)
- Per-tier configuration and isolation
- Tier statistics and monitoring

**Dependencies:**
- Requires MCPROXY-adt.1 (Cache Core) to be completed first

**Out of Scope:**
- LRU eviction policy (handled by adt-api subtask)
- Cache warming (handled by MCPROXY-010)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- 3-tier hierarchy implemented and tested
- Tier fallback logic works correctly
- Each tier can be configured independently
- Statistics available per tier

title: Cache Core - Interface and basic operations
description:
Implement core cache interface with basic get/set/delete operations.

**Scope:**
- Define CacheBackend abstract interface (get, set, delete, exists, clear)
- Implement in-memory cache backend as default
- Basic key-value storage with namespace support
- Thread-safe operations

**Out of Scope:**
- TTL management (handled by adt-ttl subtask)
- Multi-tier architecture (handled by adt-tiers subtask)
- LRU eviction (handled by adt-api subtask)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- CacheBackend interface defined in cache.py
- InMemoryCache implementation passes all tests
- Basic operations (get/set/delete) work correctly
- Namespace isolation verified

title: Simplify search: remove depth levels, make behavior automatic
description:
## Current Problem

Search has 4 depth levels (0-3) with parameters: max_depth, brief, max_tools

Now that schemas are removed from search (commit 5a4f543), depth levels feel unnecessary:
- depth=0: Server names only
- depth=1: Server names + categories + tool counts  
- depth=2: All above + tool names + truncated descriptions
- depth=3: All above + full descriptions

## Proposed Solution

Simplify to automatic behavior based on query:

**Empty query:**
- Return server list with tool counts (current depth=1)
- No tool details, just discovery

**Query provided:**
- Return matching tools with truncated descriptions (current depth=2)
- Limit to 5 tools per server by default (current max_tools default)

## Implementation

1. Remove parameters: max_depth, brief, max_tools
2. Simplify ManifestQuery.search() to automatic behavior
3. Update handlers.py to remove parameters from schema
4. Update help text
5. Test with agents to ensure discoverability remains good

## Benefits

- Simpler API for agents to understand
- Fewer parameters = less confusion
- Predictable behavior (no need to understand depth levels)
- Still covers all use cases (server discovery vs tool search)

title: Intelligent Caching System - Multi-tier cache (global + session + namespace) with TTL control, LRU eviction, and cache management API
description:
CONTEXT: Flagship feature of v4.0 - intelligent caching to accelerate agent workflows by avoiding redundant tool calls.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Multi-tier cache architecture:
  * Global cache (shared across sessions)
  * Session cache (agent-specific, isolated)
  * Namespace-level isolation
- TTL management:
  * Per-namespace TTL limits (trading: 60s, docs: 3600s)
  * Global default_ttl: 300s, max_ttl: 86400s
  * Agents can override via cache_ttl parameter
- Cache API: cache.clear(), cache.invalidate(), cache.stats()
- LRU eviction policy
- Memory backend for v4.0 (Redis in v4.1)

DEPENDENCIES: None (foundational)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Cache hits reduce tool calls by >50% in typical workflows
- Session isolation prevents cross-agent cache leaks
- TTL enforcement prevents stale data
- Stats API shows hit rate, size, entries per namespace
- Config validated on startup

ESTIMATED: 3 weeks (most complex v4.0 feature)

title: v4.0 Planning Document - Create comprehensive implementation plan with phase breakdown, timeline, and design decisions
description:
CONTEXT: This task creates the comprehensive v4.0 implementation roadmap based on our sequential thinking session.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Define 3 implementation phases (alpha/beta/rc)
- Document design decisions from our discussion:
  * Cache: Hybrid (global + session + namespace), memory backend for v4.0, per-namespace TTL limits
  * Think Engine: Multiple engines with auto-trigger on dangerous operations
  * Budgets: Multi-dimensional limits to prevent agent abuse
  * Backward compatibility: Full v3.x compat, no breaking changes
- Timeline: 10 weeks (5+3+2)
- Risk assessment for each phase
- Success criteria for each milestone

DEPENDENCIES: None (this is the planning phase)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Phase breakdown documented
- All 27 features assigned to phases
- Timeline realistic
- Design decisions captured
- Risk mitigation strategies defined

title: v4.0 Planning Document - Create comprehensive implementation plan with phase breakdown, timeline, and design decisions
Intelligent Caching System - Multi-tier cache (global + session + namespace) with TTL control, LRU eviction, and cache management API
Fix home_assistant tool descriptions
description: ha_read_file shows wrong description (linter errors instead of file reading). Need to check home_assistant MCP server configuration.
close_reason: Tool description issue is in external home_assistant MCP server, not mcproxy. Needs to be fixed upstream in the MCP server package.

title: Optimize steering texts for MCProxy v3.1
Remove mcproxy_sequence and simplify to sync-only execution
Implement sync=True mode in sandbox runtime
Refactor SandboxExecutor to Async + IPC (Unix Sockets)
Plugin System - Allow custom middleware, transforms, and extensions via plugin architecture
description:
CONTEXT: Enable community contributions and custom extensions without modifying core code.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Plugin hooks:
  * before_tool_call(tool, params) → modify params
  * after_tool_call(tool, result) → transform result
  * on_error(tool, error) → custom error handling
  * on_cache_hit(key, value) → cache transformation
- Plugin discovery: scan plugins/ directory
- Plugin config: plugins.my_plugin.enabled = true, .config = {...}
- Plugin API: register_hook(), get_config(), log()
- Security: plugins run in sandboxed subprocess

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Can write plugin without modifying core
- Plugins load on startup
- Hooks called at correct times
- Plugin errors don't crash proxy
- Performance impact minimal

ESTIMATED: 2 weeks
ROI: VERY HIGH (enables ecosystem, community contributions)

title: Secret Management Integration - Integrate with HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, etc. for secure credential storage
description:
CONTEXT: Production deployments need secure credential management, not hardcoded secrets in config.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Integrations: HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, GCP Secret Manager
- Config: secrets.backend = 'vault', vault.url = '...', vault.path = 'mcproxy'
- Secret interpolation: ${vault:api_key} in config → fetched from Vault
- Auto-rotation: detect expiring secrets, refresh automatically
- Caching: cache secrets in memory with TTL
- Audit: log secret access

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Secrets fetched from Vault on startup
- No secrets in config files
- Auto-rotation works
- Audit log shows access
- Falls back gracefully if Vault unavailable

ESTIMATED: 1 week
ROI: HIGH (production security requirement)

title: Predictive Prefetching - Analyze tool call patterns to prefetch likely-needed data before agent requests it
description:
CONTEXT: Agents often follow predictable patterns (fetch price → analyze → trade). Prefetching can eliminate latency.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Pattern analysis: track tool call sequences (A → B → C)
- Predict: after A called, prefetch B with 80% confidence
- Learning: build Markov chain of tool transitions
- Config: prefetch.enabled=true, .confidence_threshold=0.7
- Metrics: track prefetch hit rate (prefetched data was used)
- Smart cache: prefetched data has lower TTL (might not be used)

DEPENDENCIES: Cache System (MCPROXY-adt), Adaptive TTL (MCPROXY-s1j)

ACCEPTANCE:
- 50% latency reduction for common patterns
- Prefetch accuracy >70%
- Doesn't waste cache on low-confidence predictions
- Metrics show effectiveness

ESTIMATED: 2 weeks
ROI: VERY HIGH (huge UX improvement, agents feel faster)

title: Batch API - Combine multiple tool calls into single request to reduce network overhead
description:
CONTEXT: Sequential tool calls have network latency overhead. Batching can 3-5x throughput.

REQUIREMENTS:
- API: mcproxy(action='batch', calls=[
    {server: 's1', tool: 't1', params: {...}},
    {server: 's2', tool: 't2', params: {...}}
  ])
- Execution: run all calls in parallel (if independent)
- Response: [{status, result, error}, ...] in same order
- Atomic option: all-or-nothing (rollback on failure)
- Budget: batch counts as N tool calls against budget
- Timeout: max(timeout_per_call) for entire batch

DEPENDENCIES: Budget System (MCPROXY-5is)

ACCEPTANCE:
- 3-5x faster for batch operations
- Independent calls run in parallel
- Clear error handling (which call failed)
- Budget tracking correct

ESTIMATED: 1 week
ROI: HIGH (big performance win for common use case)

title: Distributed Tracing - Trace requests across multiple tool calls with timing, dependencies, and error tracking
description:
CONTEXT: When agents execute complex workflows (10+ tool calls), debugging failures and optimizing performance is extremely difficult without visibility into the full request chain.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Assign trace ID to each execute() call
- Track: tool name, params, duration, status, errors
- Visualize: trace timeline showing sequential/parallel calls
- Export: OpenTelemetry format for Jaeger/Zipkin
- API: trace.get(trace_id) → full trace with spans
- Auto-capture: all tool calls within execute() are spans
- Performance impact: <2% overhead

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Can view complete trace of agent workflow
- Timing shows bottlenecks
- Errors have full context (which tool failed, why)
- Can export to Jaeger for visualization
- Performance overhead acceptable

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks
ROI: VERY HIGH (10x faster debugging, optimization insights)

title: Adaptive TTL Learning - Track cache hits/misses/staleness to auto-recommend and adjust TTL values per tool
description:
CONTEXT: Create self-optimizing cache that learns optimal TTL from usage patterns.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Track per tool: cache_hits, cache_misses, hit_rate, staleness_events, avg_response_time
- TTL adjustment strategies:
  * Hit rate based: hit_rate > 0.8 → TTL * 1.5
  * Staleness aware: staleness_rate > 5% → TTL * 0.5
  * Cost aware: response_time > 1s → TTL * 2.0
- Combined learning: weighted average of strategies (40% hit_rate, 40% staleness, 20% cost)
- Learning modes: conservative (±10%), aggressive (±50%), hybrid
- Config: adaptive_ttl.enabled=true, .mode='hybrid', .min_ttl=30, .max_ttl=86400

DEPENDENCIES: Cache System (MCPROXY-adt)

ACCEPTANCE:
- TTL recommendations accurate
- Auto-adjustment improves cache efficiency
- Learning doesn't cause oscillation
- Can disable auto-adjust, just show recommendations

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks

title: Budget System - Multi-dimensional budgets (tool calls, think calls, execution time, cache entries) to prevent agent abuse
description:
CONTEXT: Prevent agents from exhausting resources through infinite loops, recursive calls, or abuse.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Multi-dimensional budgets:
  * max_tool_calls: 1000 per session
  * max_think_calls: 50 per session
  * max_execution_time_secs: 300 per session
  * max_cache_entries: 500 per session
  * max_parallel_calls: 10 concurrent
  * max_recursion_depth: 5 nested
- Budget tracking: session.budget_tracker.check_and_increment()
- Hard limits: reject when exceeded
- Budget reporting: budget.status() returns usage/limits
- Configurable per namespace: namespaces.trading.max_tool_calls = 500

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Budgets prevent runaway agents
- Clear error messages: "Budget exceeded: tool_calls (used 1001/1000)"
- Budget stats available via API
- No false positives (legitimate use not blocked)

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks

title: Built-in Reasoning Engine - Think parameter with configurable engine (sequential_thinking, think_tool, atom_of_thoughts) and auto-trigger for dangerous operations
description:
CONTEXT: Enable agents to reason through complex operations before executing, reducing errors.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Think parameter: mcproxy(action='execute', code='...', think=True)
- Configurable engine: think_engine.default = 'sequential_thinking'
- Multiple engines: {'sequential': 'sequential_thinking', 'simple': 'think_tool', 'decompose': 'atom_of_thoughts'}
- Auto-trigger on dangerous operations:
  * Keywords: ['delete', 'drop', 'remove', 'production', 'prod']
  * Complexity threshold: >3 tool calls
  * Configurable: think_engine.auto_think.keywords, .complexity_threshold
- Allow override: think=False to skip auto-think

DEPENDENCIES: Sequential thinking MCP server available

ACCEPTANCE:
- Think engine executes before dangerous operations
- Can use different engines: think='simple'
- Auto-trigger detects keywords correctly
- Can disable with think=False
- Think results inform execution (logged)

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Token-Based Authentication - Per-namespace/group tokens to lock sessions and prevent namespace hopping
description:
CONTEXT: Security feature to prevent agents from bypassing namespace restrictions by changing headers.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Config schema: namespaces.trading.token = 'secret-abc123'
- Token resolver: build reverse map token -> (type, name)
- X-Token header locks session to namespace/group scope
- Validate all X-Namespace headers against token scope
- 401 Unauthorized for invalid tokens
- 403 Forbidden for namespace mismatches
- Opt-in: only namespaces with tokens require auth

DEPENDENCIES: None (independent security feature)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Token prevents namespace hopping
- Clear error messages for auth failures
- Backward compatible (namespaces without tokens remain open)
- Config validated on startup (no duplicate tokens)

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks (security needs careful testing)
NOTE: Consider shipping as v3.2 before v4.0 if security is urgent

title: Fuzzy Search + Auto-Inspect - Fuzzy tool matching with auto-inspect on errors for self-correcting tool usage
description:
CONTEXT: Combine fuzzy matching with schema inspection to help agents self-correct errors automatically.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Search parameter: fuzzy=True returns first match if only 1 result
- Auto-inspect on error: if tool call fails, return schema + usage hints
- Combined flow: search(fuzzy=True) → if fail → auto-inspect → retry
- Error includes: tool schema, parameter hints, usage example

DEPENDENCIES: Enhanced Search (MCPROXY-acm), Fuzzy Matching (MCPROXY-dhz)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Fuzzy search returns single match automatically
- Failed tool calls include schema for debugging
- Reduces retry attempts by 60%+
- Works seamlessly without agent intervention

ESTIMATED: 1 week (builds on P2 tasks)

title: Fuzzy Tool Name Matching - Suggest correct tool names on errors with Levenshtein distance matching
description:
CONTEXT: Reduce agent frustration when tool names are misspelled or guessed incorrectly.

REQUIREMENTS:
- On tool not found error, calculate Levenshtein distance to all available tools
- Return top 3 suggestions with confidence scores
- Threshold: only suggest if distance < 0.4 (configurable)
- Error format: "Tool 'serch' not found. Did you mean 'search'? (confidence: 0.87)"

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Typos get helpful suggestions
- No false positives (bad suggestions)
- Response time < 50ms (don't slow down errors)
- Works across all namespaces

ESTIMATED: 3 days

title: Enhanced Search with Schemas - Include inputSchema and auto-generated usage examples in search results
description:
CONTEXT: Improve agent self-correction by showing tool schemas in search results, reducing trial-and-error.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Search results include full inputSchema for each tool
- Auto-generate usage example from schema:
  * Example: api.server('wikipedia').search(query='python')
  * Based on required parameters + common patterns
- Config: search.include_schema = true (default)
- Support brief mode (schema omitted) for quick discovery

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Search results show schema + usage example
- Examples are syntactically correct
- Reduces tool call errors by 50%+ (measured in testing)
- Backward compatible (existing searches work unchanged)

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Cache Backend Abstraction - Design pluggable cache interface to support memory/Redis/file backends
description:
CONTEXT: Enable future cache backend flexibility without rewriting cache logic.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Abstract CacheBackend interface with methods: get(), set(), delete(), clear(), stats()
- MemoryBackend implementation for v4.0 (in-memory dict with TTL tracking)
- Design for future: RedisBackend, FileBackend
- Config: cache.backend = 'memory' | 'redis' | 'file'
- Backend-agnostic cache API (higher layer doesn't know backend)

DEPENDENCIES: Cache System (MCPROXY-adt)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Interface documented with type hints
- MemoryBackend passes all cache tests
- Switching backends requires only config change
- Performance characteristics documented per backend

ESTIMATED: 1 week (infrastructure work)

title: Cache Backend Abstraction - Design pluggable cache interface to support memory/Redis/file backends
Enhanced Search with Schemas - Include inputSchema and auto-generated usage examples in search results
Fuzzy Tool Name Matching - Suggest correct tool names on errors with Levenshtein distance matching
Token-Based Authentication - Per-namespace/group tokens to lock sessions and prevent namespace hopping
Built-in Reasoning Engine - Think parameter with configurable engine (sequential_thinking, think_tool, atom_of_thoughts) and auto-trigger for dangerous operations
Fuzzy Search + Auto-Inspect - Fuzzy tool matching with auto-inspect on errors for self-correcting tool usage
Budget System - Multi-dimensional budgets (tool calls, think calls, execution time, cache entries) to prevent agent abuse
Fix brief parameter truthiness bug
description: String 'False' is truthy in Python, causing brief mode to activate unintentionally. Need proper boolean validation.
close_reason: Fixed by adding proper string-to-boolean conversion for brief parameter. Now correctly handles string values like 'false' and '0'.

title: Add search result limiting options
description: Add max_results and brief parameters to search action for better token efficiency

title: Implement tool inspection (.inspect() method)
Update documentation and examples for new features
Webhook/Event System - Push notifications when tools complete, fail, or data changes
description:
CONTEXT: Enable real-time integrations without polling.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Event types: tool.completed, tool.failed, cache.invalidated, budget.exceeded
- Webhook config: webhooks = [{event: 'tool.completed', url: 'https://...', secret: '...'}]
- Payload: {event, timestamp, session_id, tool, result, signature}
- Retry: exponential backoff on failure (max 5 retries)
- Signature: HMAC-SHA256 for webhook verification
- Filtering: webhooks only fire for specific tools/namespaces

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Webhooks fire on events
- Payloads correctly formatted
- Retries work on failure
- Signatures verify correctly
- Can filter by event type

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks
ROI: MEDIUM-HIGH (enables real-time integrations)

title: Tool Composition - Define reusable tool chains (macros) that combine multiple tools into single operation
description:
CONTEXT: Common patterns like fetch → transform → store are repeated across agents. Composition allows defining once, reusing everywhere.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Config: compositions = {
    'fetch_and_store': {
      'steps': [
        {server: 'api', tool: 'fetch', params: {...}},
        {server: 'db', tool: 'store', params: {'data': '[0].result'}}
      ]
    }
  }
- Parameter substitution: .input, [0].result
- Call: api.composition('fetch_and_store', input='...')
- Error handling: stop on first failure, return partial results
- Nesting: compositions can call other compositions

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Compositions execute steps in order
- Parameter substitution works
- Error handling clear
- Can debug step-by-step
- Reusable across sessions

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks
ROI: HIGH (DRY principle, reduces agent code)

title: Circuit Breakers - Auto-detect failing services and temporarily stop routing requests to prevent cascading failures
description:
CONTEXT: When a downstream service fails, continuing to send requests wastes resources and can exhaust connection pools.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Circuit states: CLOSED (normal) → OPEN (failing) → HALF_OPEN (testing)
- Open circuit: fail fast with clear error (no actual call)
- Config: circuit_breaker.failure_threshold=5, .timeout=60
- Auto-recovery: try again after timeout (HALF_OPEN)
- Metrics: track circuit state changes, time spent in OPEN
- Manual override: force_open/force_close via API

DEPENDENCIES: Tool Health Tracking (MCPROXY-173)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Failing services don't exhaust resources
- Auto-recovery when service heals
- Clear errors: "Circuit OPEN for server X (5 consecutive failures)"
- Can override manually

ESTIMATED: 3 days
ROI: HIGH (prevents cascading failures, improves resilience)

title: Prometheus Metrics Export - Export standard metrics (cache hit rate, tool latency, errors) in Prometheus format for monitoring
description:
CONTEXT: Production deployments need monitoring. Prometheus is the de facto standard.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Endpoint: GET /metrics (Prometheus text format)
- Metrics:
  * mcproxy_cache_hits_total, mcproxy_cache_misses_total
  * mcproxy_tool_calls_total{server, tool, status}
  * mcproxy_tool_duration_seconds{server, tool}
  * mcproxy_active_sessions, mcproxy_active_connections
  * mcproxy_budget_exhausted_total{type}
- Labels: namespace, server, tool, status
- Config: metrics.enabled=true, .path='/metrics'

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Prometheus can scrape /metrics
- All key metrics exposed
- Standard labels for filtering
- Low overhead (<1% performance impact)

ESTIMATED: 3 days
ROI: HIGH (enables production monitoring, low effort)

title: Tool Health Tracking - Track success rate, latency percentiles, last errors per tool for observability
description:
CONTEXT: Provide visibility into tool reliability and performance for debugging.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Track per tool:
  * success_rate (rolling 24h window)
  * latency_p50, latency_p95, latency_p99
  * last_10_errors with timestamps
  * total_calls, total_failures
- Expose via inspect: mcproxy(action='inspect', server='wikipedia', include_health=true)
- Health-based routing: skip tools with success_rate < 0.5
- Config: health.tracking.enabled = true, health.window_hours = 24

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Health metrics accurate
- Latency percentiles calculated correctly
- Last errors visible
- Can disable health-based routing

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Connection Pool + Cache Integration - Size connection pool dynamically based on cache hit rate and TTL patterns
description:
CONTEXT: Optimize resource usage by correlating connection pool behavior with cache effectiveness.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Connection pool sizing based on cache hit rate:
  * hit_rate > 0.8 → pool_size * 0.5 (cache serves most requests)
  * hit_rate < 0.2 → pool_size * 2.0 (need more connections)
- TTL-informed connection lifetime:
  * Keep connection alive for avg_ttl * 1.2
  * Close idle connections when cache is hot
- Predictive connection warming:
  * Pre-warm connections for cache entries expiring soon
  * Warm if hit_rate > 0.5 and expiry < 60s
- Metrics: pool.stats() shows cache correlation

DEPENDENCIES: Cache System (MCPROXY-adt), Adaptive TTL (MCPROXY-2jw)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Pool size adapts to cache behavior
- Connection lifetime correlates with TTL
- Pre-warming reduces latency
- 30-50% resource reduction in high-cache-hit scenarios

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks

title: Auto-Think Budget Awareness - Skip auto-think when think budget is low to preserve calls for critical operations
description:
CONTEXT: Prevent budget exhaustion from auto-think triggering on every operation.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Check think budget before auto-triggering
- Skip auto-think if budget.usage['think_calls'] > limits['max_think_calls'] * 0.8
- Log warning: "Think budget low (40/50 used), skipping auto-think"
- Still allow explicit think=True calls (agent controls budget)
- Configurable threshold: think_engine.auto_think.budget_threshold = 0.8

DEPENDENCIES: Budget System (MCPROXY-5is), Think Engine (MCPROXY-5vi)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Auto-think respects budget limits
- Explicit think calls still work
- Warning logged when skipped
- Critical operations can still use think budget

ESTIMATED: 3 days

title: Parameter Normalization - Accept both snake_case and camelCase parameter names based on schema
description:
CONTEXT: Reduce agent errors from parameter name mismatches (thought_number vs thoughtNumber).

REQUIREMENTS:
- Inspect tool schema to detect parameter naming convention
- Accept both snake_case and camelCase variants
- Normalize to schema's expected format before tool call
- Log when normalization applied: "Normalized 'thought_number' to 'thoughtNumber'"
- Performance: cache schema inspection results

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Both parameter formats work
- No ambiguity (don't normalize if both exist)
- Clear logging of normalization
- <5ms overhead per tool call

ESTIMATED: 3 days

title: Dry Run + Validation - Preview execution plan without running, validate syntax/schema/permissions
description:
CONTEXT: Let agents preview operations before executing, especially for dangerous operations.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Parameter: dry_run=True
- Dry run returns:
  * Tools that would be called
  * Parameters for each call
  * Estimated execution time
  * Validation status (syntax, schema, permissions)
- Validation only: validate_only=True (faster, no execution plan)
- Combined with think: dry_run=True, think=True → reason about plan before executing

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Dry run shows accurate execution plan
- Validation catches errors before execution
- No side effects from dry run
- Works with all tools

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Audit Trail - Structured logging with user/reason/ticket metadata for compliance and debugging
description:
CONTEXT: Enable compliance and debugging by tracking who did what and why.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Parameter: audit={'user': 'alice', 'reason': 'prod debug', 'ticket': 'JIRA-123'}
- Audit log format (JSON):
  * timestamp, session_id, namespace, tool, params, result, duration_ms, audit_metadata
- Config: audit.enabled = true, audit.log_file = '/var/log/mcproxy_audit.jsonl'
- Search audit logs: audit.search(user='alice', start='2025-03-01')
- PII handling: mask sensitive params (passwords, tokens)

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- All tool calls logged with metadata
- Audit logs searchable
- PII properly masked
- Performance impact <5%

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Cache Invalidation - Auto-invalidate related cache entries on mutations (POST/DELETE) with dependency tracking
description:
CONTEXT: Keep cache fresh by invalidating entries when underlying data changes.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Detect mutation operations: tool names containing 'write', 'delete', 'update', 'create'
- Dependency tracking: api.server('db').write() invalidates db.read() cache
- Config: cache.invalidation.mutation_keywords = ['write', 'delete', 'update', 'create']
- Cache tags: group related entries, invalidate by tag
  * Example: cache.set(key, value, tag='db_queries')
  * cache.clear(tag='db_queries')
- Manual invalidation: cache.invalidate(server='db', tool='read')

DEPENDENCIES: Cache System (MCPROXY-adt)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Mutations invalidate related cache entries
- Tag-based invalidation works
- No false invalidations (over-invalidation)
- Configurable mutation detection

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Result Size Limits - Truncate large outputs with smart summarization to prevent context overflow
description:
CONTEXT: Prevent agents from overwhelming their context window with huge tool outputs.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Parameter: max_result_size=50000 (bytes, default 50KB)
- Truncation strategy:
  * Strings: truncate at word boundary, add "... (truncated, 500KB total)"
  * Lists: show first 100 items, add "... (9900 more items)"
  * Dicts: show first 50 keys, add "... (450 more keys)"
- Smart summarization: detect JSON/logs and summarize intelligently
- Config: cache.max_result_size_bytes = 50000

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Large outputs don't crash agents
- Truncation preserves important info (beginning/end)
- Clear indication of truncation
- Configurable per-call: max_result_size=100000

ESTIMATED: 4 days

title: Rate Limiting - Token bucket rate limits per tool/namespace with configurable strategies (queue/reject/retry)
description:
CONTEXT: Prevent agents from overwhelming external APIs or services.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Token bucket algorithm per tool
- Config: rate_limits.sequential_thinking = {tokens_per_second: 0.5, burst_size: 5}
- Strategies:
  * queue: wait until token available (default)
  * reject: fail immediately with 429 Too Many Requests
  * retry: retry with exponential backoff
- Namespace-level limits: namespaces.trading.rate_limit = {max_calls_per_minute: 60}
- Rate limit headers in responses: X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Rate limits prevent API abuse
- All strategies work correctly
- Rate limit info visible in responses
- Configurable per tool/namespace

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Adaptive TTL Learning - Track cache hits/misses/staleness to auto-recommend and adjust TTL values per tool
Connection Pool + Cache Integration - Size connection pool dynamically based on cache hit rate and TTL patterns
Auto-Think Budget Awareness - Skip auto-think when think budget is low to preserve calls for critical operations
Cache Invalidation - Auto-invalidate related cache entries on mutations (POST/DELETE) with dependency tracking
Result Size Limits - Truncate large outputs with smart summarization to prevent context overflow
Parameter Normalization - Accept both snake_case and camelCase parameter names based on schema
Rate Limiting - Token bucket rate limits per tool/namespace with configurable strategies (queue/reject/retry)
Audit Trail - Structured logging with user/reason/ticket metadata for compliance and debugging
Dry Run + Validation - Preview execution plan without running, validate syntax/schema/permissions
Tool Health Tracking - Track success rate, latency percentiles, last errors per tool for observability
Enhance error messages with hints
description: Add contextual hints when common mistakes occur (e.g., accessing result structure incorrectly)
close_reason: Addressed via documentation - response structure now explicitly documented in AGENTS.md with examples. Runtime hint injection would add complexity without significant benefit.

title: Auto-Parallelization - Concurrent Executor
description:
Implement concurrent execution engine for parallel tool calls.

**Scope:**
- Execute independent call batches concurrently
- Use asyncio.gather() for parallel execution
- Collect and aggregate results
- Error handling for partial failures

**Dependencies:**
- Requires MCPROXY-f6t.2 (Independent Call Detection) to be completed first

**Out of Scope:**
- Static analysis (handled by f6t-1 subtask)
- Dependency detection (handled by f6t-2 subtask)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Concurrent execution engine implemented
- Parallel execution working with asyncio
- Result aggregation correct
- Partial failure handling robust

title: Auto-Parallelization - Independent Call Detection
description:
Implement algorithm to detect independent tool calls that can be parallelized.

**Scope:**
- Analyze dependency graph for independent operations
- Group independent calls into parallel batches
- Handle conditional dependencies
- Estimate performance gains from parallelization

**Dependencies:**
- Requires MCPROXY-f6t.1 (Static Analyzer) to be completed first

**Out of Scope:**
- Static analysis (handled by f6t-1 subtask)
- Concurrent execution (handled by f6t-3 subtask)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Independent call detection algorithm implemented
- Batch grouping working correctly
- Conditional dependency handling
- Performance estimation accurate

title: Auto-Parallelization - Static Analyzer
description:
Implement static analysis and dependency graph builder for code analysis.

**Scope:**
- Parse Python code to extract tool calls
- Build dependency graph of tool operations
- Identify variable dependencies between calls
- Detect side effects and mutations

**Out of Scope:**
- Independent call detection (handled by f6t-2 subtask)
- Concurrent execution (handled by f6t-3 subtask)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Static analyzer implemented
- Dependency graph builder working
- Tool call extraction accurate
- Side effect detection functional

title: Natural Language - Orchestration and Safety
description:
Implement orchestration layer and safety mechanisms for natural language API.

**Scope:**
- Orchestration pipeline (intent → match → params → execute)
- Safety checks before execution (dangerous operations)
- User confirmation prompts for destructive actions
- Error handling and recovery

**Dependencies:**
- Requires MCPROXY-ygh.1 (Intent Extraction)
- Requires MCPROXY-ygh.2 (Tool Matching)
- Requires MCPROXY-ygh.3 (Parameter Generation)

**Out of Scope:**
- Individual pipeline components (handled by ygh-1, ygh-2, ygh-3)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Orchestration pipeline implemented
- Safety checks for dangerous operations
- User confirmation flow working
- Error handling and recovery tested

title: Natural Language - Parameter Generation
description:
Implement parameter generation from natural language context.

**Scope:**
- Extract parameters from natural language context
- Map context to tool schema parameters
- Parameter validation against schema
- Handle missing parameters with prompts

**Out of Scope:**
- Intent extraction (handled by ygh-1 subtask)
- Tool matching (handled by ygh-2 subtask)
- Orchestration and safety (handled by ygh-4 subtask)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Parameter extraction module implemented
- Schema validation for generated parameters
- Missing parameter detection and prompting
- Test coverage for parameter generation

title: Natural Language - Tool Matching
description:
Implement tool matching based on extracted intent.

**Scope:**
- Match intents to available tools using search API
- Handle ambiguous matches (multiple tools)
- Tool ranking based on intent confidence
- Support for tool disambiguation prompts

**Out of Scope:**
- Intent extraction (handled by ygh-1 subtask)
- Parameter generation (handled by ygh-3 subtask)
- Orchestration and safety (handled by ygh-4 subtask)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Tool matching algorithm implemented
- Ambiguity detection and handling
- Tool ranking based on relevance
- Test coverage for matching scenarios

title: Natural Language - Intent Extraction
description:
Implement intent extraction from natural language commands.

**Scope:**
- Design intent schema (action, target, modifiers)
- LLM integration for intent classification
- Intent validation and normalization
- Support for common intent patterns (search, execute, inspect)

**Out of Scope:**
- Tool matching (handled by ygh-2 subtask)
- Parameter generation (handled by ygh-3 subtask)
- Orchestration and safety (handled by ygh-4 subtask)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Intent extraction module implemented
- LLM prompts designed for intent classification
- Intent schema defined and documented
- Test coverage for common intent patterns

title: Tool Versioning - Support multiple versions of same tool with gradual migration support
description:
CONTEXT: Tools evolve over time. Need to support old and new versions simultaneously during migration.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Config: servers.wikipedia = {command: '...', version: 'v2'}
- Version aliasing: versions = {'v1': 'wikipedia_v1', 'v2': 'wikipedia_v2'}
- Default version: default_version = 'v2'
- Call specific version: api.server('wikipedia:v1').search()
- Migration tracking: track which clients use which version
- Deprecation: mark version as deprecated, log warnings

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Multiple versions coexist
- Can call specific version
- Migration tracking works
- Deprecation warnings clear
- Performance impact minimal

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks
ROI: MEDIUM (simplifies migrations)

title: Natural Language API - Accept natural language commands and auto-translate to tool calls using LLM
description:
CONTEXT: Some users prefer natural language over learning tool APIs. This lowers the barrier to entry.

REQUIREMENTS:
- API: mcproxy(action='nl', command='get latest bitcoin price and save to database')
- LLM integration: use configured LLM to parse intent → tool calls
- Steps: 1) Extract intent, 2) Find tools, 3) Generate params, 4) Execute
- Safety: require confirmation for dangerous operations
- Feedback: show translation before executing
- Config: nl.enabled=true, .llm_server='perplexity_sonar', .confirm_dangerous=true

DEPENDENCIES: LLM MCP server configured

ACCEPTANCE:
- Natural language commands execute correctly
- Translations are accurate (90%+)
- Dangerous operations require confirmation
- Clear feedback on what will be executed
- Falls back to manual tool selection if unsure

ESTIMATED: 3 weeks
ROI: VERY HIGH (broader adoption) but RISKY (LLM reliability)
NOTE: Consider as experimental feature in v4.2

title: Streaming Results - SSE streaming for long-running tools instead of blocking
description:
CONTEXT: Improve UX for long-running tools by streaming results instead of blocking.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Parameter: stream=True
- Tools that support streaming return SSE events:
  * event: progress, data: {percent: 50, message: 'Processing...'}
  * event: result, data: {final_result}
- Timeout handling: stream doesn't count against execution timeout
- Cancellation: client can cancel streaming via SSE disconnect
- Config: streaming.enabled = true, .max_stream_time = 3600

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Streaming works for compatible tools
- Progress updates visible
- Can cancel streaming
- Non-streaming tools still work

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks

title: Adaptive Timeouts - Use historical execution data (avg + 2σ) for intelligent timeout calculation
description:
CONTEXT: Reduce timeout errors by using realistic timeouts based on actual performance.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Track execution time per tool (rolling window)
- Calculate: timeout = avg_time + 2 * std_dev
- Config: adaptive_timeouts.enabled = true, .min_timeout = 5, .max_timeout = 300
- Override: timeout_secs parameter still works (manual control)
- Expose: show calculated timeout in tool schema

DEPENDENCIES: Tool Health Tracking (MCPROXY-173)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Timeouts adapt to actual performance
- Fewer timeout errors
- Can still override manually
- Outliers don't skew calculations

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Context Propagation - Pass user intent and urgency hints to context-aware tools
description:
CONTEXT: Enable tools to adapt behavior based on user intent and urgency.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Parameter: context={'intent': 'debug production issue', 'urgency': 'high', 'user': 'alice'}
- Pass context to tools that support it (via MCP protocol extension)
- Tools can use context to:
  * Adjust verbosity (debug → more logs)
  * Prioritize requests (high urgency → faster response)
  * Customize output format
- Config: context_propagation.enabled = true

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Context passed to compatible tools
- Tools can access context
- Non-compatible tools ignore context
- Privacy: sensitive context not logged

ESTIMATED: 3 days

title: Fallback Chains - Automatic failover to backup servers with load balancing strategies
description:
CONTEXT: Improve reliability by failing over to backup servers when primary fails.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Config: servers.wikipedia.fallback = {
    'chain': ['wikipedia_backup1', 'wikipedia_backup2'],
    'strategy': 'failover' | 'round_robin' | 'load_balance',
    'health_check_interval': 60
  }
- Failover: try next server in chain on failure
- Round robin: distribute load across chain
- Load balance: route to least loaded server
- Health checks: skip unhealthy servers
- Metrics: track which server handled request

DEPENDENCIES: Tool Health Tracking (MCPROXY-173)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Failover works transparently
- All strategies work correctly
- Health checks prevent routing to dead servers
- Metrics show server usage

ESTIMATED: 1.5 weeks

title: Mock Mode - Inject mock tool responses for testing without hitting real services
description:
CONTEXT: Enable testing and development without accessing production services.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Parameter: mock={'prod__dangerous_call': {'result': 'mocked', 'status': 'ok'}}
- Mock responses returned instead of calling real tool
- Mock mode indicator in responses: 'mocked': true
- Config: mock.enabled = true, mock.responses = {...}
- Recording mode: record real responses for future mocking
  * mock.record = ['wikipedia__search'] → saves responses to file

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Mocked responses returned correctly
- No real tool calls in mock mode
- Recording saves responses
- Can mix mocked and real tools

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Auto-Parallelization - Static analysis to detect independent tool calls and execute them concurrently
description:
CONTEXT: Speed up execution by automatically running independent tool calls in parallel.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Parameter: auto_parallel=True
- Static analysis:
  * Build dependency graph from code
  * Identify independent calls (no shared variables)
  * Execute independent calls concurrently
- Example: a = server1.tool(); b = server2.tool() → parallel
- Example: a = server1.tool(); b = server2.tool(a) → sequential
- Budget awareness: respect max_parallel_calls limit

DEPENDENCIES: Budget System (MCPROXY-5is)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Independent calls run in parallel
- Dependent calls remain sequential
- Speedup >2x for independent workloads
- Budget limits respected

ESTIMATED: 2 weeks (complex static analysis)

title: Session Variables - Cross-call state persistence with session.set()/session.get() API
description:
CONTEXT: Enable agents to persist state across multiple tool calls in a session.

REQUIREMENTS:
- API:
  * session.set('key', value) → stores in session
  * session.get('key') → retrieves from session
  * session.delete('key') → removes from session
  * session.clear() → clears all variables
- Use case: session.set('user_id', api.server('auth').login())
- Scope: session-scoped (isolated per X-Session-ID)
- Persistence: in-memory for v4.0, optional Redis in v4.1
- Expiration: session vars expire with session (timeout or explicit close)

DEPENDENCIES: Session management infrastructure (already exists)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Variables persist across calls
- Session isolation works
- API intuitive and documented
- Performance overhead minimal

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Cache Warming - Config-based pre-population of expensive queries on startup
description:
CONTEXT: Reduce cold-start latency by pre-loading cache with predictable, expensive queries.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Config schema:
  * cache.warming.enabled = true
  * cache.warming.queries = [
      {server: 'coinmarketcap', tool: 'get_prices', params: {...}, ttl: 300}
    ]
- Warm on startup (async, non-blocking)
- Warm on config reload
- Track warming status: cache.warming_status() → {completed: 5, failed: 1, pending: 0}
- Retry failed warming with backoff

DEPENDENCIES: Cache System (MCPROXY-adt)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Warm queries execute on startup
- Non-blocking (server ready immediately)
- Status visible via API
- Failed queries don't crash startup

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Schema Migration - Auto-migrate deprecated tool calls to new APIs with parameter mapping
description:
CONTEXT: Handle tool API changes gracefully without breaking existing agent code.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Config: schema_migrations = {
    'old_api__legacy_call': {
      'new_tool': 'new_api__modern_call',
      'param_map': {'old_param': 'new_param', 'deprecated_param': null}
    }
  }
- On deprecated tool call:
  * Log warning: "Tool 'legacy_call' is deprecated, migrating to 'modern_call'"
  * Apply parameter mapping
  * Execute new tool
- Version tracking: track which tools are deprecated
- Grace period: allow deprecated calls for N days

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE:
- Deprecated tools still work
- Parameter mapping applied correctly
- Warnings logged
- Can configure grace period

ESTIMATED: 1 week

title: Cache Warming - Config-based pre-population of expensive queries on startup
Schema Migration - Auto-migrate deprecated tool calls to new APIs with parameter mapping
Fallback Chains - Automatic failover to backup servers with load balancing strategies
Context Propagation - Pass user intent and urgency hints to context-aware tools
Mock Mode - Inject mock tool responses for testing without hitting real services
Adaptive Timeouts - Use historical execution data (avg + 2σ) for intelligent timeout calculation
Auto-Parallelization - Static analysis to detect independent tool calls and execute them concurrently
Session Variables - Cross-call state persistence with session.set()/session.get() API
Streaming Results - SSE streaming for long-running tools instead of blocking
